data.title: Kroger Is Maintained at Outperform by Telsey Advisory Group
Investors Urged to Contact Levi & Korsinsky for Information Before July 22, 2025 - Organon & Co. (OGN)
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / June 20, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Organon & Co. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/organon-co-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=153725&wire=1&utm_campaign=5

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Organon & Co. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between October 31, 2024 and April 30, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: According to the complaint, defendants provided overwhelmingly positive statements to investors while, at the same time, disseminating materially false and misleading statements and/or concealing material adverse facts concerning the true state of Organon's priorities, particularly, related to capital allocation through quarterly dividends. Notably, defendants concealed the high priority of Organon's debt reduction strategy following the Company's acquisition of Dermavant, resulting in a 70% decrease for the regular quarterly dividend.

Following this news, the price of Organon's common stock declined dramatically. From a closing market price of $12.93 per share on April 30, 2025, Organon's stock price fell to $9.45 per share on May 1, 2025, a decline of more than 27% in the span of just a single day.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Organon stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/organon-co-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=153725&wire=1&utm_campaign=5 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.title: Rockfall on popular Canadian hiking trail kills one, injures three
data.description:
Canadian rescue officials were due to resume searching on Friday for survivors after a major rockfall crashed onto a popular Rocky Mountain hiking trail, killing at least one person and injuring three others.

The accident happened on Thursday near the Bow Glacier Falls in Banff National Park, about 225 km (140 miles) northwest of Calgary, Alberta. The area is known for its natural beauty and is particularly busy in summer.

Local resident Niclas Brundell, who was hiking on the trail, estimated that about 15 to 20 people were standing to the right of the falls when he and his wife noticed small rocks tumbling down. Alarmed, the two of them ran from the area.

"I turned round and saw basically a whole shelf of a mountain come loose," he told the Canadian Broadcasting Corp.

"We just kept sprinting and I couldn't see the people behind us anymore because they were all in that cloud of rock."

In a joint statement issued late on Thursday, the Royal Canadian Mounted Police and Parks Canada said one person had died at the scene and three others were taken to hospital.

Search and recovery operations, involving drones and dogs, would resume early on Friday, it said.

Andy Dragt, an organiser for a local hiking club, told the Globe and Mail newspaper that one of its members had been killed in the rockfall. Around 10 club members had been present, he said, declining to identify the dead person.

data.title: Euro Area Consumer Confidence Edges Down in June
data.description:
The euro area's consumer confidence index stood at -15.3 points, down from the revised -15.1 points previously, according to European Commission data published Friday.

The latest reading is below the consensus estimate of -14.5 points.

data.title: Gun maker Smith & Wesson plunges after lower sales, profits as tariffs weigh
data.description:
** Shares of Smith & Wesson Brands down 18% to $8.93, over 2-mth low, early Fri after reporting lower sales and earnings amid tariff uncertainty

** SWBI shares on course for biggest daily pct drop since 20.3% dive on Dec 6

** Maryville, Tennesse-based co late Weds said Q4 net sales fell nearly 12% y/y and adj EPS more than halved to 20 cents, with CEO Mark Smith citing macroeconomic factors pressuring discretionary spending

** On the earnings call, CFO Deana McPherson said with near-term demand remaining soft, co believes Q1 sales could be down approx 10% from last year

** Current qtr margins also to be lower "due to promotions and increased costs due to tariffs on raw materials that are capacity constrained in the United States, such as steel," she added

** Shares of rival Sturm, Ruger falling 3.7% on the session to $36.35

** With move on Fri, SWBI shares down ~11% YTD

** RGR shares up ~3% in 2025, including move on Fri

data.title: Dutch launch operation Orange Shield to keep NATO summit safe
data.description:
The Netherlands has launched operation Orange Shield to protect next week's NATO summit, deploying combat helicopters, sea patrols, over 10,000 military and police personnel, bomb squads and air defence systems.

About 6,000 delegates and leaders from 32 countries, including U.S. President Donald Trump, French President Emmanuel Macron, and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, will attend the closed-off event at The Hague’s World Forum.

The Dutch navy will deploy seven frigates as part of the country's largest security operation.

"The aim is to observe everything that's happening in the area, at sea, and in the air," commander of the Tromp frigate Dennis Oorburg told Reuters.

Dutch counterterrorism chief Pieter-Jaap Aalbersberg told Dutch broadcaster NOS that cybersecurity risks were also being closely monitored.

Heads of state and ministers from NATO countries will arrive at Schiphol Airport on Monday and be escorted to The Hague via a secure highway corridor.

On Tuesday, Dutch King Willem-Alexander will host a state dinner for NATO leaders. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy, who is set not to attend the summit, has also been invited to the dinner but has not yet confirmed his attendance.

The summit officially begins on Wednesday, with NATO boss Mark Rutte aiming to get consensus from leaders on a 5% defence spending target, demanded by Trump. But Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez has opposed the higher target.

Protests are expected around the summit, including a planned highway blockade by Extinction Rebellion environmental activists and demonstrations against the war in Gaza and LGBTI+ hate speech. Police say protests will be allowed "within the limits of the law".

data.title: Market Chatter: Tesla to Start Opening India Showrooms in July
data.description:
Tesla is set to start opening its showrooms in India in July as its first batch of vehicles has arrived in the country, Bloomberg reported Friday, citing certain documents and people familiar with the discussions.

The electric vehicle maker plans to open its first showroom in Mumbai around mid-July, followed by another showroom in New Delhi, sources told Bloomberg.

The Model Y vehicles shipped to India were produced in China, and the company also imported components, merchandise and spare parts from the US, China and the Netherlands, according to the report.

Tesla did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

data.title: ICE Canola Rising Friday morning
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--ICE canola futures were stronger Friday morning, touching fresh highs as Chicago soyoil resumed its uptrend after yesterday's Juneteenth holiday in the United States.

Tight old crop supplies and weather uncertainty for the new crop remained supportive. European rapeseed and Malaysian palm oil were both higher on the day.

Weekly canola exports reported by the Canadian Grain Commission of 131,400 tonnes were down 17 per cent on the week.

However, crop year-to-date movement at 8.8 million tonnes compares with only 5.7 million tonnes at the same point the previous year.

While some technical signals are looking overbought, speculators are still holding large net long positions in canola.

About 19,900 canola contracts had traded as of 8:45 CDT.

Prices in Canadian dollars per metric ton at 8:45 CDT:

 
           Price      Change 
Jul       750.50     up 5.20 
Nov       748.70     up 4.70 
Jan       756.70     up 4.40 
Mar       759.80     up 1.90

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1015 ET - CarMax is aiming to improve its operational efficiency and reduce its costs. "A key driver of these efficiency gains and experience enhancements has been our strategic deployment of AI technology across our operations," Finance Chief Enrique Mayor-Mora says on a call with analysts. The used-car retailer launched an AI-powered virtual assistant during the recent quarter, which resulted in a 30% improvement in containment rate, he notes. (A chatbot containment rate measures how many customer inquiries can be resolved using the virtual agent in place of a live agent.) "We see tremendous opportunity to continue expanding AI applications across our business to drive both the top line growth and operational excellence," Mayor-Mora says. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

0948 ET - Thyssenkrupp Nucera's acquisition of key technology assets from Green Hydrogen Systems is likely to boost its innovation capabilities and market share, Mwb Research's Leon Muhlenbruch writes in a note. "Despite current headwinds in the global hydrogen sector, tk nucera is strategically positioning itself for the anticipated rebound," he says. By acquiring GHS's assets rather than the full company, the company reduces its exposure to operational risks, he adds. The move will allow the construction engineering company to gain valuable intellectual property and a testing facility with an operational prototype, he says. Shares are up 2.6% at 8.76 euros. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0948 ET - CarMax CEO Bill Nash says on a call with analysts that tariff uncertainty sparked an uptick in demand for used cars. Demand picked up around the end of March and continued accelerating in April, according to Nash, who notes that April was CarMax's strongest month during the recent quarter. "And I would just go back to--even before we saw that initial uptick--the business was growing," Nash says. He notes the company's efforts to improve operations and customer experiences are bearing fruit, and that the company expects to grow sales and gain share for the remainder of the year. Shares rise 2.4%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

0939 ET - Demand for new euro-denominated credit remains tight, Societe Generale's Juan Valencia says in a note. New issuance is also high but it's not enough to satiate demand, he says. New euro corporate bonds supply has amounted to 72.3 billion euro so far in June, he says. "We expect volumes to start slowing down gently in the next three weeks." (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0924 ET - Bank of Montreal's acquisition of Burgundy Asset Management isn't monumental but it's a step in a strategic direction, Desjardins Doug Young says. The C$625 million acquisition of the wealth manager "is not material to BMO's financials," but it is "part of management's plan to tilt the bank toward higher-return on equity businesses." BMO is looking to push its ROE toward 15% over the medium term, and wealth management is a high-ROE business, says Young. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0843 ET - Darden Restaurants is seeing steady demand from consumers. The owner of Olive Garden and other restaurant chains reports higher 4Q sales, benefiting in part from higher same-restaurant sales. Darden notes that same-restaurant sales for Olive Garden grew 6.7% and while LongHorn Steakhouse grew 6.7%, among others disclosed. This lines up with findings from UBS earlier this month, who said they expected U.S. restaurant industry sales to improve throughout the year after a tough start and noted that Olive Garden and other brands accounted for a sizable portion of growth in the category. Shares tick up 3.3% to $230.02 in pre-market trading. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

0800 ET - Empire had a strong 4Q, but a broader economic slowdown in Canada could weigh on the grocer. Desjardins' Chris Li says that he forecasts a 9% growth in EPS for fiscal 2026, in between management's 8%-11% range, backed by food same-store sales growth. But this assume a stable and/or improving macro environment that support a higher basket and traffic at its full-service banners. A return to more a cautious consumer could see customers choose the cheaper alternatives or generally buying less as they tighten their budgets. "The key risk is weakening economic conditions causing another wave of trade-down to discount," Li says. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0741 ET - Flutter Entertainment is well-positioned to face the competitive challenge from prediction markets, Peel Hunt analysts Ivor Jones and Douglas Jack write in a note. Prediction markets are a gambling product and will be regulated as such, they say. The U.S. federal regulation for these markets represents both an opportunity and a threat for the parent company of FanDuel and other gaming and gambling brands, they add. However, "Flutter has the resources to compete effectively as market regulation becomes clear," they say. Shares closed at $272.47 on Thursday. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0723 ET - Gold futures fall, and are on track for a weekly loss. Futures are down 1.1% at $3,371.40 a troy ounce. Gold has also failed to benefit from heightened geopolitical tensions as the U.S dollar emerged as the market's preferred safe-haven for now, Tradu.com's Nikos Tzabouras says in a note. The precious metal's rally of 24% in the year to date appears to be showing fatigue as the U.S. Federal Reserve looks reluctant to cut interest rates, benefiting the dollar, Tzabouras says. Investor enthusiasm for non-interest bearing bullion is typically damped by higher interest rates. That said, gold is likely to remain the preferred risk-off safe-haven asset as the de-dollarization trend continues over the longer term, Tzabouras writes. The path to fresh all-time highs is still open, he adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0706 ET - BlackBerry has a low bar for the upcoming 1Q results, which should help in coming in ahead of expectations and raise targets for the year, RBC's Paul Treiber says. Ahead of the cybersecurity's 1Q release on Tuesday, the analyst says BlackBerry's FY26 guidance was on the conservative side, but the environment has improved and "been more stable than generally expected." Treiber thinks there may be grounds for an upgrade in guidance for the year as well by as much as 4% to 13% which in turn may boost EPS guidance by 10c-12c from 8c-10c previously "given likely more stable renewals and incremental license revenue." (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0700 ET - The dollar has room to weaken moderately further as U.S. policy uncertainty remains a burden, HSBC's Paul Mackel says in a note. "We are sceptical in thinking that it will be calmer going forward when many trade discussions are ongoing and deals are light in detail." Another source of uncertainty is U.S. fiscal policy, particularly questions around potentially taxing global investors, he says. Meanwhile, structural de-dollarization is showing some semblance of growing importance. Departure from the dollar should continue, however it isn't the easiest currency to leave when other currencies have flaws such as weak growth and low yields, he says. HSBC expects the euro to rise to $1.20 in the fourth quarter from $1.1524 currently. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0629 ET - U.K. fintechs have clear room for more growth, UBS says in a research note. Firms like Chase UK, Monzo, Oaknorth, Revolut, Shawbrook and Starling are driven by deposits and transactions. They have been investing in marketing, product expansion, customer acquisition and new regional offices to boost growth, analyst Jason Napier and his team write. Despite these expenses, they have delivered an average return on tangible equity of 17.8%, compared with 15.3% in 2023 and an average 13.2% for large U.K. banks. Fintechs also achieved 55% on-year statutory profit growth in 2024, with loans up 15% and deposits rising 32%. They have aggregate market shares between 1% and 5% and are set to keep on winning ground. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: FQTI's Quantum Technology Secures Order With Bharat Electronics
data.description:
ORLANDO, FL / ACCESS Newswire / June 20, 2025 / Fonon Quantum Technologies, Inc. ("FQTI"), a technology conglomerate specializing in quantum, defense, additive manufacturing, and semiconductor technologies, announced today that its subsidiary, Quantum Technology, Inc., is processing an international order from Bharat Electronics Ltd. (BEL), a Bangalore-headquartered electronics company focusing on aerospace and defense industries. The multi-product purchase order was received through Photonics Marketing Company, a trusted distribution partner for Quantum Technology in India.

"We are excited to remain a trusted supplier of electro-optical components in the Indian market,"said Sanjay Adhav, President of Quantum Technology. "Photonics Marketing Company is a valued member in our distributor network overseas, connecting small businesses like Quantum Technology with some of India's finest tech companies like BEL. We hope that our products satisfy the client's need for high-quality, standard or customized HV drivers."

This order includes customized high-voltage pulse amplifiers that provide a reliable switching pulse for a variety of applications in laser technology used in conjunction with Pockels cells. This model, in the HVP-5LQ series by Quantum Technology, is an advanced, flexible, solid-state driver with 12 nanosecond (ns) rise time, adjustable positive or negative outputs to 3KV, and an adjustable high-voltage bias output to 5KV.

Quantum Technology, now under the FQTI umbrella, continues to develop high-tech EO devices for UV, infrared, and visible light technologies, applications, and research. Its integration into FQTI enhances Quantum Technology's sales channels, expands its market reach, and boosts its production capabilities, setting the stage for more innovation and growth.

About Fonon Quantum Technologies, Inc.

Fonon Quantum Technologies Inc. (FQTI) is a diversified company specializing in advanced technologies and systems in quantum, defense, additive manufacturing, and semiconductor markets. With a strong foundation in R&D and equipment design, FQTI serves a wide array of industries through its subsidiaries: Fonon Technologies Inc., Fonon Media Corp., Quantum Technology Inc., and Beamer Laser Marking Systems. Learn more at www.fonon.com
